releaseHighlights:
Chromaspace 2.0 beta is a major viewer-focused update. The viewer is now closer to a standalone analysis workspace, with more controls moved out of the OFX panel and into the viewer itself.
What’s New
- Multi-window viewer workspace: add multiple plots, resize them, and arrange them into split, triple, quadrant, or six-view layouts.
- New plot types: Waveform and Histogram are now available alongside Cube, Chromaticity, Gloss View, and the existing color models.
- Per-window settings: each plot window can have its own model, camera/view, appearance, and analysis settings.
- Better viewer menu: most viewer-side controls now live inside the viewer, reducing the need to adjust OFX parameters in Resolve.
Identity and gray ramp workflow improvements: automatic resolution detection, better generator-instance communication, and clearer disabled states when required data is unavailable.
- Image lasso workflow: viewer control for Resolve’s OFX overlay lasso, with clearer prompts when the Chromaspace node needs to be selected.
- Resolve bridge support: bundled helper script improves refresh behavior, node detection, lasso coordination, and identity/ramp communication. Sadly, as this relies on Resolve's scripting API, it means Chromaspace won't be supported on the free version of Resolve anymore.
